story.iWant: to create an IAgentProvider by name string, where LLM providers are transparently wrapped into the agent interface
story.soThat: callers always receive IAgentProvider without needing to check or handle two different interfaces (IAgentProvider vs IAIProvider)

task.title: Implement AgentProviderFactory with provider registration and apiKeyRef resolution

tasks.task: - [ ] Create `packages/providers/src/agent-provider-factory.ts` - [ ] Define `IAgentProviderFactory` interface with `create()`, `register()`, `dispose()` methods - [ ] Define `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES` constant object with the four built-in provider name strings - [ ] Define `PROVIDER_NAME_PATTERN` regex `/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}$/` and `FORBIDDEN_NAMES` set - [ ] Define `API_KEY_REF_PATTERN` regex `/^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$/` for validating apiKeyRef format - [ ] Implement `resolveApiKey(entry: ProviderChainEntry): string` helper: - Returns `''` if `entry.apiKeyRef` is undefined (some providers don't need keys) - Validates apiKeyRef format (alphanumeric + underscore only) - Looks up `process.env[entry.apiKeyRef]` - Throws if env var is missing: `Environment variable "${entry.apiKeyRef}" is not set for provider "${entry.provider}"` - [ ] Define `AgentProviderFactory` class implementing `IAgentProviderFactory` with private `creators` map - [ ] Add `private locked = false` flag and private `lock(): void` method - [ ] Implement `register(name, creator)` method with provider name validation and lock checks - [ ] Implement `create(entry: ProviderChainEntry): Promise<IAgentProvider>` method: - Looks up creator by `entry.provider`, throws `Unknown provider: {name}` if not found - Calls `resolveApiKey(entry)` to get the API key from environment - Calls `initialize()` if the provider supports it, with spread order `{ ...entry.config, apiKey: resolvedKey, model: entry.model }` - Wraps initialize() errors: `Provider "${entry.provider}" failed to initialize: ${sanitizedMessage}` - Detects LLM providers by checking `'sendMessageSync' in provider` (duck-typing, see code comments) - Wraps LLM providers via `wrapAsAgent(provider, entry.model ?? 'default')` - Returns agent providers directly as `IAgentProvider` - [ ] Implement `dispose(): Promise<void>` that clears the creators map - [ ] Accept optional `logger?: ILogger` in constructor for INFO-level logging - [ ] Register built-in providers in constructor using `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES` constants - [ ] Call `this.lock()` after registering built-ins - [ ] Add thread-safety comment: not safe for sharing across worker threads

- [ ] Export `wrapAsAgent(llm: IAIProvider, model: string): IAgentProvider` function - [ ] Add limitations documentation comment: no tool-use loops, file tracking, exit code semantics - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: build a `MessageRequest` from `config.prompt` with `messages: [{ role: 'user', content: config.prompt }]` - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: use `config.model ?? model` for the request model (task-level override support) - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: call `llm.sendMessageSync(request)` to get `MessageResponse` - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: map `response.content` to `AgentTaskResult.output` - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: map `response.finishReason === 'error'` to `success: false` - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: on `sendMessageSync()` error, use safe casting: `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: set `costUsd: 0` (actual cost computed by cost-monitor) - [ ] Implement `executeTask()`: track `durationMs` using `Date.now()` delta - [ ] Implement `isAvailable()`: always return `true` with trade-off documentation comment - [ ] Implement `dispose()`: delegate to `llm.dispose()`

task.title: Wire built-in provider registrations, exports, and tests

tasks.task: - [ ] Register `'claude-code'` creator using `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES.CLAUDE_CODE` - [ ] Register `'opencode'` creator using `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES.OPENCODE` - [ ] Register `'openrouter'` creator using `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES.OPENROUTER` - [ ] Register `'zen-mcp'` creator using `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES.ZEN_MCP` - [ ] Export `AgentProviderFactory`, `IAgentProviderFactory`, `wrapAsAgent`, `resolveApiKey`, `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES` from `packages/providers/src/index.ts` - [ ] Create `packages/providers/src/agent-provider-factory.test.ts` with all verification tests - [ ] Test fixtures use `apiKeyRef: 'TEST_API_KEY'` with `process.env.TEST_API_KEY = 'test-key'` setup

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `create({ provider: 'claude-code' })` returns an `IAgentProvider` that is a `ClaudeAgentProvider` instance
`create({ provider: 'openrouter', model: 'z-ai/z1-mini', apiKeyRef: 'TEST_KEY' })` with `process.env.TEST_KEY` set returns an `IAgentProvider` wrapped via `wrapAsAgent()`
`resolveApiKey()` resolves `entry.apiKeyRef` from `process.env`, returns `''` if undefined, throws if env var missing or format invalid
`initialize()` is called with correct spread order: `{ ...entry.config, apiKey: resolvedKey, model: entry.model }` (explicit fields override user config)
`initialize()` errors are wrapped: `Provider "${name}" failed to initialize: ${sanitizedMessage}`
`wrapAsAgent().executeTask()` calls `sendMessageSync()` and maps response fields correctly; uses `config.model ?? model` for task-level overrides
`wrapAsAgent().executeTask()` on `sendMessageSync()` error returns `{ success: false, error: message }` using safe error casting
Mock `OpenCodeProvider.initialize()` throwing propagates wrapped error to caller
`ClaudeAgentProvider.initialize()` is a no-op that ignores apiKey from chain entry
Factory return type is always `IAgentProvider` -- callers never receive `IAIProvider`
`IAgentProviderFactory` interface is exported for dependency inversion (ProviderChain depends on interface)
After lock, `register()` cannot override existing providers but can add new ones
`BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES` constant is exported with all four provider names

docs.doc: Story specification with full AgentProviderFactory, IAgentProviderFactory interface, resolveApiKey(), and wrapAsAgent() code
ProviderChainEntry type definition with apiKeyRef (NOT apiKey) used by factory.create()
ProviderChain consumer of IAgentProviderFactory interface (downstream dependency)
Technical architecture, naming conventions, error handling patterns
Project coding standards, TypeScript strict mode requirements

code.file: AgentProviderFactory class, IAgentProviderFactory interface, resolveApiKey() helper, wrapAsAgent() function, BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES constant
Unit tests for factory creation, apiKeyRef resolution, wrapping, name validation, locking, dispose, and error cases
Add exports for AgentProviderFactory, IAgentProviderFactory, wrapAsAgent, resolveApiKey, BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES
OpenCodeProvider -- native IAgentProvider with eager SDK init

dependencies.dependency: ProviderChainEntry type with apiKeyRef field from configuration schema (Story 9-1)
ILogger interface for optional structured logging

constraints.constraint: Factory.create() return type is `Promise<IAgentProvider>` only -- never IAIProvider or a union
IAgentProviderFactory interface must be exported for dependency inversion; ProviderChain depends on the interface
ProviderChainEntry uses `apiKeyRef` (env var name), NOT `apiKey` (raw key). Factory resolves via `process.env[apiKeyRef]`
Provider names must match `/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}$/` and reject `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`
Built-in providers locked after construction; register() cannot override but can add new names
initialize() config uses `{ ...entry.config, apiKey, model }` so explicit fields always win
Use `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` -- never `(err as Error).message`
initialize() errors are wrapped: `Provider "${name}" failed to initialize: ${msg}` to avoid leaking sensitive content
All code must compile under TypeScript strict mode (strict: true, noImplicitAny, noImplicitReturns)
Files use kebab-case; classes use PascalCase; functions use camelCase; constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
Use .js extensions in import paths for ESM compatibility
wrapAsAgent() returns a new object implementing IAgentProvider; does not mutate the IAIProvider
Unknown provider names throw synchronously; initialize() errors are wrapped and re-thrown for ProviderChain to handle
No API keys in logs, error messages, or test fixtures; apiKeyRef format validated
ClaudeAgentProvider.initialize() must remain a no-op -- the Claude CLI manages its own authentication
OpenCodeProvider.initialize() eagerly creates an SDK client; failures are wrapped and re-thrown by the factory
AgentProviderFactory is NOT thread-safe; instantiate once per process, do not share across worker threads
wrapAsAgent() uses `config.model ?? model` to support task-level model overrides

interface.name: IAgentProviderFactory
interface.description: Factory interface with create(entry), register(name, creator), dispose(). ProviderChain depends on this, not the concrete class.

interface.name: IAgentProvider
interface.description: Agent provider contract with executeTask(config, onProgress?), isAvailable(), and dispose()

interface.name: IAIProvider
interface.description: LLM provider contract with initialize(), sendMessage(), sendMessageSync(), getCapabilities(), getModels(), dispose()

interface.name: AgentTaskConfig
interface.description: Task configuration with prompt, cwd, model?, maxBudgetUsd?, allowedTools?, permissionMode?, outputFormat?, sessionId?

interface.name: AgentTaskResult
interface.description: Task result with success, output, costUsd, durationMs, error?

interface.name: MessageRequest
interface.description: LLM request with messages[], model?, maxTokens?, temperature?, topP?, stream?, tools?, metadata?

interface.name: MessageResponse
interface.description: LLM response with id, content, model, usage (TokenUsage), finishReason, tool_calls?, metadata?

interface.name: ProviderChainEntry
interface.description: Chain entry with provider (string), model?, apiKeyRef? (env var name — resolved at runtime via process.env), config? (Record<string, unknown>). NOTE: uses apiKeyRef, NOT apiKey.

interface.name: ProviderConfig
interface.description: Provider initialization config with apiKey, baseUrl?, timeout?, maxRetries?, retryDelay?, model?, metadata?

interface.name: ILogger
interface.description: Logger interface with info(), warn(), error(), debug() methods for structured logging

standards.standard: 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, 85% function coverage
TDD: write tests before or alongside implementation
Mock all external dependencies; no real API calls or CLI spawning in unit tests
Test fixtures use apiKeyRef with process.env setup, NOT raw apiKey values

locations.location: Unit tests for AgentProviderFactory, resolveApiKey(), wrapAsAgent(), name validation, locking, and dispose

ideas.idea: Test create({ provider: 'claude-code' }) returns IAgentProvider with executeTask method
Test create({ provider: 'opencode' }) returns IAgentProvider with executeTask method
Test create({ provider: 'openrouter', model: 'z-ai/z1-mini', apiKeyRef: 'TEST_KEY' }) with process.env.TEST_KEY set returns wrapped IAgentProvider
Test create({ provider: 'zen-mcp' }) returns wrapped IAgentProvider
Test create({ provider: 'unknown' }) throws 'Unknown provider: unknown'
Test resolveApiKey() returns '' when apiKeyRef is undefined
Test resolveApiKey() returns process.env value when apiKeyRef is set
Test resolveApiKey() throws when env var is missing
Test resolveApiKey() throws on invalid apiKeyRef format (hyphens, spaces, etc.)
Test register() rejects invalid provider names (empty string, uppercase, __proto__, constructor, prototype)
Test register() after lock cannot override existing provider but can add new ones
Test initialize() called on openrouter provider with correct config spread order
Test initialize() called on zen-mcp provider with correct config
Test initialize() error is wrapped with provider name
Test ClaudeAgentProvider.initialize() is called but is a no-op (ignores apiKey)
Test OpenCodeProvider.initialize() throwing error propagates as wrapped error to create() caller
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() builds correct MessageRequest from prompt
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() uses config.model when provided (task-level override)
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() falls back to closure model when config.model is undefined
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() maps successful response (finishReason: 'stop') to success: true
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() maps error response (finishReason: 'error') to success: false
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() catches sendMessageSync error with safe casting (instanceof check)
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() handles non-Error thrown values via String(err)
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() sets costUsd to 0
Test wrapAsAgent().executeTask() tracks durationMs correctly
Test wrapAsAgent().isAvailable() always returns true
Test wrapAsAgent().dispose() delegates to llm.dispose()
Test register() adds custom provider that can be created
Test factory.dispose() clears creators map (subsequent create() throws)
Test BUILTIN_PROVIDER_NAMES has correct values for all four providers
Test IAgentProviderFactory interface is satisfied by AgentProviderFactory
